era_context: Early Dynastic Period I in Mesopotamia (c. 3150–2900 BCE). Egypt in the Early Dynastic Period (c. 3100–2686 BCE). The Indus Valley Civilization in its early phases. Writing systems emerging in multiple regions. City-states consolidating power in Mesopotamia and Egypt.
disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for 3144 BCE in any historical tradition. The year falls within a period where individual years are not reliably distinguished in the historical record.
historiographic_note: 3144 BCE is archaeologically undifferentiated from surrounding years. No written records, inscriptions, or other contemporary documentation exist for this specific year. The historical record for this period is based entirely on archaeological stratigraphy and artifact analysis, which cannot reliably distinguish individual years. Chronological assignment to this year is conventional rather than evidential.
